title: An Append-Only Record Needs A Physical Append Operation, Not An Insertion Anchored To Text
problem: Session 023's two continuity entries were committed into the middle of the old monolithic Batch 003.md — inside Session 021's closed record — instead of appended after the actual most recent session. The edit was anchored to the first '<!-- [VXG RealForever] -->' marker in the file, treating the continuity marker like an end-of-file sentinel in a code file. The record stayed structurally valid markdown, so nothing failed loudly; the damage was semantic: the two newest sessions became invisible to the documented cold-start path ('scroll to the bottom, read the most recent session'), were mis-attributed to an older session by a different instance, and carried none of the required session metadata (date, instance, thread).
exploration: rely on the written rule alone ('scroll to the bottom, append') — already existed and did not prevent the mistake, because an instance editing a 94KB file it has only partially read will anchor on the first plausible marker it can see

reverse the ordering (newest session at the top) so recent context is at a known position — rejected: prepending still requires a mid-file insertion anchor, which is the exact operation that failed, and it makes concurrent-session merge conflicts collide at the same line
restructure so the append-only rule has a physical shape the file system enforces: one file per session inside a batch directory, date-prefixed filenames for chronological ordering
decision: Batches are directories from batch-003/ onward, one YYYY-MM-DD-session-0NN.md file per session. Logging a new session is a file creation — an operation with no insertion anchor to miss and no closed record it can disturb. The date prefix makes 'ls' order chronological, so 'the most recent session' is the last filename and time-based filtering is a filename comparison, not a content parse. lib/session-bootstrap.js resolves and reports the newest session file mechanically.
lesson: A convention that says 'append only' but is implemented as 'edit a shared file at the right spot' will eventually be violated by any writer that anchors its edit on searchable text — the same hazard class as sentinel-based tools corrupting themselves. When a record must be append-only, choose a storage shape where appending is the only physically natural operation (new file in a directory beats new block in a file) and where the ordering key lives in metadata the file system already sorts (a date-prefixed filename), not in position within a document.
impact: The misplacement failure mode is structurally impossible for new sessions: closed sessions are closed files. Session 023 was relocated with corrected attribution and a provenance note rather than silently rewritten. Regression-tested in tests/14-session-bootstrap.test.js (latestSessionFile unit tests plus an integration test binding the registry row to a real directory and a real newest session file).
